purpose: Single-image full card sheet prompt for one complete champion deck
important_note: This prompt asks for complete playable-looking cards, not only card artwork. It includes frames, cost gems, name banners, rules boxes, type labels, rarity gems, and monster stats where needed.

project.title: The Fracture of Aetherfall
project.playstyle: complex combos, polarity switching, buffs, magnetic control, chain reactions, overclocking, controlled destruction

image_request.generate_as: one single image containing 9 complete card designs
image_request.do_not_generate: separate images per card or plain artwork-only panels

layout.outer_margin: consistent dark background margin around the sheet
layout.alignment: all cards aligned to the same grid, same scale, same proportions

must_include_on_every_card: mana cost gem in the top left
readable card name on a banner
monster_card_extra_requirements: attack value jewel in the bottom left
health value jewel in the bottom right
non_monster_card_requirements: use a small spell-type emblem at the bottom instead

card_frame_direction.overall_layout: original fantasy digital card game layout inspired by classic collectible card readability, but not a direct copy of any existing game
card_frame_direction.frame_material: brushed dark metal frame with floating magnetic fragments, brass screws, arcane glass, blue-white electric seams, small gears, and polished inventor details
card_frame_direction.cost_gem: top-left blue arcane crystal with a large clear white number
card_frame_direction.name_banner: engraved metal-and-brass nameplate under or overlapping the artwork window, with high-contrast readable text
card_frame_direction.art_window: large vertical illustration area occupying the top half of the card
card_frame_direction.rules_box: clean pale parchment or brushed ivory metal panel in the lower third, with short readable black/dark text
card_frame_direction.rarity_gem: small centered gem below the artwork or above the rules box, color-coded by rarity

rarity_colors.legendary: large bright blue-white diamond with gold trim

card_frame_direction.typography: large readable fantasy card typography, clean and simple, no tiny paragraphs, spell every card name and rule exactly as provided

global_art_direction.style: consistent stylized high fantasy card illustration, painterly but clean, game-ready, cinematic lighting
global_art_direction.rendering: same rendering style across all 9 cards, strong silhouettes, readable actions, detailed but not noisy
global_art_direction.lighting: cool blue-white arcane light with warm brass highlights and controlled electric sparks
global_art_direction.character_consistency: Geert should feel like the same kind-hearted arcane inventor across the sheet whenever he appears: clever, focused, approachable, wearing practical tinkerer gear with brass tools, metal gauntlets, blue-white energy lenses, and magnetic devices orbiting around him.
global_art_direction.deck_identity: The whole sheet should communicate controlled complexity: polarity rings, attraction and repulsion, chain reactions, clever machinery, precise destruction, and a sense that every chaotic effect is engineered on purpose.

grid_order.rules_text: Deal 2 damage. Set Polarity to Positive.
grid_order.art_prompt: Geert launches a glowing magnetic anchor bolt from a brass-and-steel device. Blue-white sparks pull floating metal shards into a positive polarity ring around the target.

grid_order.rules_text: Gain 3 Block. Set Polarity to Negative.
grid_order.art_prompt: Geert braces behind a circular repulsion field as metal fragments and incoming debris are pushed away from him. The energy ring glows blue-white with a negative polarity symbol.
A compact arcane magnetic drone hovers in a workshop sky, made from polished metal plates, tiny gears, blue glass lenses, and crackling polarity coils. It looks agile and engineered.
A controlled magnetic field pulls scattered metal fragments, runic cards, and tiny components into a perfect orbit around Geert's device. The composition should feel clever and precise.

grid_order.rules_text: Deal 3. If Polarity changed, hit another enemy for 2.
grid_order.art_prompt: A charged capacitor tower releases a branching chain of blue-white lightning between floating metal nodes. Geert adjusts a dial as the chain reaction leaps with deliberate control.
Geert pushes a glowing machine beyond safe limits. Cards, sparks, and blueprints whirl around him while red warning heat glows inside the device. It feels powerful but risky.
Geert flips a brass lever between positive and negative polarity while two opposing magnetic rings rotate around him. A compact defensive shield forms from pulled-together metal plates.
A towering construct made from magnetized scrap, brass plates, gears, and glowing blue-white coils stands protectively in front of Geert. Heavy guard posture, engineered strength.

grid_order.rules_text: Deal 4 to all enemies. If Polarity changed, repeat.
grid_order.art_prompt: Geert stands at the center of a flawless machine-circle while magnetic rings, lightning chains, drones, and metal fragments trigger a perfectly synchronized chain reaction across the battlefield.

master_prompt: Create one single portrait-format image showing a 3 by 3 sheet of 9 complete fantasy game cards for The Fracture of Aetherfall. These must be full usable card mockups, not plain artwork tiles. Each card must have an ornate original card frame, top-left mana cost gem, readable name banner, large illustration window, rules text box, type ribbon, rarity gem, and consistent Geert deck styling. Use an original fantasy digital card layout inspired by classic collectible card readability, but do not copy any existing card game frame exactly. The deck is Geert, the Polarity Architect, a Tinkerer / Arcane Artificer champion of Control. Visual identity: brushed dark metal, floating magnetic fragments, brass screws, gears, arcane glass, blue-white electric seams, controlled sparks, polished steel, cyan polarity rings, clean inventor details, dark workshop shadows, pale ivory rule panels. Make all 9 cards aligned in the same 3 columns by 3 rows grid, same scale, same proportions, with even gutters and dark neutral background around them. Use readable fantasy card typography and spell the following names, costs, and rules exactly. Row 1 column 1: cost 1, Spark Anchor, Attack, common, text: Deal 2 damage. Set Polarity to Positive. Artwork: Geert launches a glowing magnetic anchor bolt from a brass-and-steel device while blue-white sparks pull metal shards into a positive polarity ring. Row 1 column 2: cost 1, Repulsion Pulse, Skill, common, text: Gain 3 Block. Set Polarity to Negative. Artwork: Geert braces behind a circular repulsion field as metal fragments and incoming debris are pushed away. Row 1 column 3: cost 2, Magnetic Drone, Drone, common, text: Positive: +1 Attack. This is a monster card, with attack 2 bottom left and health 3 bottom right. Artwork: a compact arcane magnetic drone hovers, made from polished metal plates, tiny gears, blue glass lenses, and crackling polarity coils. Row 2 column 1: cost 2, Attraction Field, Power, uncommon, text: Gain 2 Combo Charge. Negative: draw 1. Artwork: a controlled magnetic field pulls metal fragments, runic cards, and tiny components into orbit around Geert's device. Row 2 column 2: cost 2, Chain Capacitor, Attack, uncommon, text: Deal 3. If Polarity changed, hit another enemy for 2. Artwork: a charged capacitor tower releases branching blue-white lightning between floating metal nodes while Geert adjusts a dial. Row 2 column 3: cost 1, Overclock, Skill, rare, text: Draw 2 cards. Take 2 damage. Artwork: Geert pushes a glowing machine beyond safe limits, with cards, sparks, blueprints, and red warning heat. Row 3 column 1: cost 1, Polarity Switch, Reaction, uncommon, text: Flip Polarity. Gain 2 Block. Artwork: Geert flips a brass lever between positive and negative polarity while two opposing magnetic rings rotate around him. Row 3 column 2: cost 4, Scrap Colossus, Construct, rare, text: Guard. Positive: gain 4 Block. This is a monster card, with attack 4 bottom left and health 6 bottom right. Artwork: a towering construct made from magnetized scrap, brass plates, gears, and glowing blue-white coils stands protectively. Row 3 column 3: cost 5, Perfect Chain Reaction, Ultimate, legendary, text: Deal 4 to all enemies. If Polarity changed, repeat. Artwork: Geert stands at the center of a flawless machine-circle while magnetic rings, lightning chains, drones, and metal fragments trigger a perfectly synchronized chain reaction. Important: one single image, exactly 9 complete cards, no extra cards, no separate outputs.
negative_prompt: plain artwork-only panels, missing card frames, missing mana costs, missing name banners, missing rules boxes, separate images, extra cards, wrong grid, text outside the cards, unreadable tiny text, random nonsense text, misspelled names, watermark, logo, direct Hearthstone copy, direct Magic the Gathering copy, photorealistic product photo, anime style, modern sci-fi frame, guns, modern clothing, cropped cards, inconsistent card sizes, inconsistent frames, stone giant theme, mossy troll theme
production_warning: Image generators can still make imperfect text. For production-quality playable cards, the best workflow is to generate the card visuals and render the final text, costs, and stats in the browser UI. This prompt is optimized for full visual mockups and first-pass card assets.
